commit.message:
Add README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: pre-release cleanup for v0.5.7

- Remove deleted !include click-to-preview feature from README
- Add SIGKILL fallback to stopLocalServer after SIGTERM timeout
- Add try/catch to handleCrash dialog callback
- Add JSDoc to isMermaidFile/resolveMermaidPath
- Add @param/@returns to puml-preview.ts functions
- Fix theme file palette variable comments
- Clarify CHANGELOG v0.5.7 wording and v0.5.6 terminology

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: invite-only auth with Discord OAuth + security hardening (#52)

* feat: project x-ray — 8 upgrades across observability, type safety, AI reliability, and security

- Enable Workers Logs observability (wrangler.toml, 100% sampling)
- Eliminate 43 `as unknown as` D1 casts with typed .first<T>()/.all<T>() generics
- Centralize game balance + AI constants (src/config/game-balance.ts, ai-parameters.ts)
- Structured JSON logging across 55 files (~90 console calls → {event, component, ...})
- D1 batch() for parallel queries in 8 locations (~22 round trips → 8)
- Gemini native structured output (responseJsonSchema) for 20 AI call sites
- Zod request validation middleware for 9 API handlers
- Clean 612 duplicate/merge-artifact files
- Full x-ray inventory + upgrade docs (docs/x-ray/)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add RateLimiterDO — Durable Object rate limiter replacing in-memory store

Sliding window algorithm using DO storage with alarm-based cleanup.
Replaces ephemeral in-memory rate limiting that reset on every cold start.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: upgrade TypeScript 5.7→5.9.3, jose 6.1→6.2, bump compat date, wire wrangler types --check

- TypeScript 5.9.3: import defer support, strictInference, conditional narrowing
- jose 6.2.0: security patches
- Compat date 2026-02-24: deleteAll() now clears DO alarms
- wrangler types --check added to pre-merge gate
- Regenerated worker-configuration.d.ts from config

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: migrate test infra from MockD1Database to real Miniflare D1

Replace better-sqlite3 mock with Miniflare's workerd D1 for production-
accurate SQL behavior in tests. Key changes:

- Add test/miniflare-env.js: process-singleton Miniflare with real D1/KV/R2
- Rewrite test/setup.js: plain-function AI mock (bypasses vitest spy leaks),
  mockReset() for vi.fn mocks, real D1 in beforeEach
- Set isolate: true + maxForks: 1 in vitest.config.js (100% deterministic,
  eliminates cross-file mock pollution from isolate: false)
- Convert 26 test files from db.exec(interpolated SQL) to prepare().bind().run()
- Delete test/mocks/d1-database.js and better-sqlite3 dependency

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: adopt CF getByName API, Discord modal components, context menus, perf timing

- Migrate all 32 DO stub sites from idFromName()+get() to getByName()
- Add performance.now() high-res timing to AI router, Gemini shim, image generator, middleware
- Add Discord modal Radio/Checkbox Groups (types 18/21/22/23) with character class selection
- Add 3 context menu commands: Inspect Character, Challenge to Duel, Extract Lore
- Add extractModalValues() helper handling Label-wrapped modal components
- Migrate vitest.config.js from poolOptions to top-level maxWorkers/minWorkers (Vitest 4)
- Update all test DO mocks with getByName method

1330/1330 tests pass, 113 files green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: expand context menus, enhance bio modal, add confirmation dialogs

Context Menus (5 new, 8 total):
- Trade With Player, View Faction, Spectate Player (user commands)
- Bookmark Story, Quote in Action (message commands)

Bio Modal overhaul:
- Add RadioGroup for biography focus (heritage/appearance/motivation/secret/personality)
- Add CheckboxGroup for personality traits (brave/cautious/honorable/cunning/compassionate/ambitious)
- Traits passed to DO and stored for AI narration context

Confirmation dialogs:

Other:

1330/1330 tests pass, 113 files green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add pagination, autocomplete expansion, and Activity player endpoint

Pagination for 5 list commands (market browse, faction list, quest list,
craft list, base status) using the existing paginate() system with
page handlers registered in components.ts.

Autocomplete added to 4 commands: dungeon explore (room connections),
pvp accept/attack (match IDs), leaderboard skills (GURPS skill names),
season claim (unclaimed rewards). Registration script updated.

New GET /api/world/:id/player endpoint for Discord Activity — returns
character stats, inventory, portrait in the shape the Activity frontend
expects.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: lint fixes, test setup getByName mocks, formatting cleanup

Prettier auto-formatting for context-menus.ts and modals.ts.
Test setup and fixture files updated with getByName DO mock method.
Achievement manager and progression manager test expansions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: invite-only auth system with Discord OAuth, admin provisioning, security hardening

- Invite-only: signup returns 403, admin provisions users with temp passwords
- Discord OAuth standalone flow: /callback page exchanges code for JWT
- Discord whitelist middleware: empty table = open, populated = whitelist-only
- Admin endpoints: create-user, reset-password, list-users, whitelist CRUD
- Security fixes: isAdmin via BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_IDS env (was granting admin to all),
  change tokens one-time-use via iat vs last_password_change, meta.changes checks
  on changePassword/adminResetPassword, crypto.getRandomValues for temp passwords,
  error logging on JWT/token verification
- Frontend auth DAG: Landing → Discord OAuth or Login → ChangePassword → Dashboard
- ProtectedRoute redirects to /login, /signup redirects to /
- Fixed authSignIn to send {login} not {email}
- Migration 0050: must_change_password, discord_whitelist, password_reset_tokens
- Custom domain route + ALLOWED_ORIGINS for unstablepiggy.mock1ngbb.com

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(organize): handle junk directories in empty dir cleanup

.Spotlight-V100 and .fseventsd are directories on macOS but were only
checked in the is_file() branch, causing parent directories to be
treated as non-empty. Split _JUNK_FILES into files and dirs, add
cross-platform junk dirs (macOS/Windows/NAS), and use shutil.rmtree
for recursive junk directory removal.

A standalone financial management dashboard for insurance claims tracking:

commit.message:
Add Ted Variance benchmark vs Knutson's published results + persistent eval caching

Ted Benchmark:
- New /ted-benchmark page comparing our Ted model on EPL/Championship
  to Knutson's published hit rates (StatsBomb xG vs our Understat xG)
- Gap analysis with interpretation (xG quality vs implementation)
- Side-by-side grade comparison, home/away breakdown, ROI tracking
- EPL + Championship support added to football-data-uk.ts

Eval Caching:
- Completed seasons (2023-24, 2024-25) cached forever on disk + Blob
- Current season cached for 24h (matches are weekly)
- ?refresh=true to force recalculation after model changes
- Blob persistence survives Vercel deploys

feat: add dev error overlay with Vite integration and frame dimming (#45)

Integrate with Vite's built-in error overlay (ssrFixStacktrace + hot.send)
for source-mapped stack traces in the browser. Classify errors by pipeline
phase (module-transform, middleware, access, render, handler). Dim framework-
internal frames in terminal output, show application frames at normal brightness.
Extract React component stacks from render errors.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(hooks): resolver loop infinito de reinicio del commander por conflictos 409 (#1367)

Causa raíz: cleanStaleProcesses() en restart-operational-system.js parseaba
el lockfile JSON con parseInt() → NaN → concluía "PID muerto" → borraba el
lockfile de un commander VIVO. El launcher detectaba "no lockfile" → lanzaba
otro commander → conflicto 409 con Telegram → crash → ciclo infinito.

Cambios:
- restart-operational-system.js: parsear lockfile como JSON antes de parseInt
  fallback, y skip si no se puede determinar PID (nunca borrar lockfile válido)
- telegram-commander.js: killOtherCommanders() al arrancar — usa wmic para
  encontrar y matar procesos telegram-commander.js previos antes de tomar el
  lock, eliminando definitivamente los conflictos 409

Co-authored-by: QA Tester <qa@intrale.test>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add private GitHub repo support for paid skill submissions

Sellers can now authenticate via GitHub OAuth with `repo` scope to submit
paid skills from private repositories. Tokens are captured during OAuth
callback, stored securely in a separate table with no public RLS policies,
and threaded through the entire import pipeline.

- Add user_github_tokens table with RLS (migration)
- Capture provider_token at OAuth callback
- Request `repo` scope for GitHub OAuth
- Thread user token through import-skill, storage, submissions
- Use GitHub API contents endpoint for private repo file access
- Improve error messages (401/403/404 with actionable guidance)
- Add private repo info box on submit form

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Move embedding pipeline to server-side using Supabase.ai.Session

The client-side @xenova/transformers model loading was failing because
HuggingFace CDN returns HTML instead of model JSON, causing parse errors.
Moved the entire extract→chunk→embed→store pipeline to the edge function
using Supabase's built-in gte-small embedding model (Supabase.ai.Session).

- Edge function now handles full document processing via /process-document
- Client only uploads file + creates record, then calls server pipeline
- Smart sentence-aware chunking runs server-side
- Embeddings generated with built-in Supabase.ai.Session('gte-small')
- Added safe response parsing (text first, then JSON.parse with try/catch)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: case-insensitive address lookup — checksum addresses returned empty tx data (#18)

* feat(etherscan): add tokenbalance, getminedblocks, getLogs, supply, price actions

Add five missing Etherscan-compatible API actions needed by third-party tools:
- module=account&action=tokenbalance: ERC-20 token balance for an address
- module=account&action=getminedblocks: blocks produced by a validator
- module=logs&action=getLogs: event logs with block range and topic filters
- module=stats&action=ethsupply: total QFC supply
- module=stats&action=ethprice: QFC price structure (zeroed, no oracle)

Add professional error handling with try-catch blocks

Implemented comprehensive error handling as per industry best practices:

- Added try-catch blocks to all service methods
- Integrated NestJS Logger for structured logging
- Added JSDoc comments and contextual logging
- Updated unit tests to use custom exceptions
- Created ERROR_HANDLING.md documentation

All tests passing (24 unit + 29 E2E)
Requested by user as their professional standard

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix(config): enforce allow_edit_existing_files restriction in tool selection (#388) (#389)

Wire up the existing but unused `infer_task_edit_requirement()` heuristic
to the tool selection pipeline. When a prompt indicates editing intent
(fix, implement, refactor, etc.), tools with `allow_edit_existing_files =
false` are now filtered out during tier-based selection.

Three changes:
- `resolve_tool_and_model()` accepts `needs_edit` and forwards it to
  `resolve_tier_tool_rotated()` (was hardcoded `false`)
- New `resolve_tier_tool_filtered()` replaces the unfiltered fallback,
  `resolve_tier_tool()` now delegates to it with `needs_edit=false`
- `run_cmd_execute` calls `infer_task_edit_requirement()` and passes
  the result through the strategy resolution chain

Closes #388

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: v1.1.36 — PhaseSpec.skip_condition for scratchpad-driven phase skipping

Add SkipCondition dataclass and PhaseSpec.skip_condition field enabling
workflows to skip phases based on scratchpad state at dispatch time.
Skipped phases produce no tasks; downstream phases inherit the dependency
chain unchanged (Airflow none_failed / Argo when-expression semantics).

- domain/phase_strategy.py: SkipCondition dataclass (stdlib only),
  PhaseSpec.skip_condition field, WorkflowPhaseStatus.mark_skipped() /
  is_resolved(), _evaluate_skip() helper, expand_phases_from_specs()
  updated with scratchpad parameter
- phase_executor.py: expand_phases / expand_phases_with_status updated;
  SkipCondition and _evaluate_skip re-exported via shim
- web/schemas.py: SkipConditionModel Pydantic schema,
  PhaseSpecModel.skip_condition field
- web/routers/workflows.py: _to_domain_skip_condition() adapter,
  build_workflows_router(scratchpad=) parameter
- web/app.py: pass _scratchpad to build_workflows_router
- 44 new tests, 2728 total pass
- Demo 21/21 checks PASSED (phase-b skipped, A+C completed, workflow=complete)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove X close button from lightbox overlay

Fullscreen button, backdrop click, and Escape key handle closing.

The device stops searching for balls after a shot completes, requiring
manual re-triggering. This adds automatic re-activation of ball detection
after club metrics are received (or when no club data is available),
so the device seamlessly starts searching for the next ball.

commit.message:
feat: Migrate DuckDB to Supabase PostgreSQL

Replace DuckDB with psycopg2 for all database operations. The pipeline
now writes directly to a cloud-hosted Supabase PostgreSQL instance,
eliminating file-locking issues and enabling shared access with one-lot-ai.

- config.py: DATABASE_URL from env replacing DB_PATH
- schema.py: psycopg2 connection and DDL
- nse_collector.py: cursor pattern, %s placeholders, ON CONFLICT upserts
- main.py: all queries updated for psycopg2
- requirements.txt: duckdb → psycopg2-binary + python-dotenv

commit.message:
Real data integrity validation: cross-validate national vs city, required fields, logical checks

- validateDataQuality now checks: required fields, min thresholds, national minimums
- crossValidateNationalVsCity: compares national totals vs Monterrey (catches "first state only" bug)
- Required field checks: city-profile needs ages/hh/pop, demographics needs core DENUE fields
- National minimums: architects>1000, tileStores>1000, ferreterias>5000 (would have caught the bug)
- Percentage sanity: male+female must sum to ~100
- Health check now reports dataIntegrity: PASS/FAIL separately from HTTP pass/fail
- WhatsApp alerts include integrity warnings
- Boot check logs integrity status

Fix fullscreen button by using event delegation for click handlers

Element-based click listeners can fail when DOM nodes are moved between
containers. Switched to document-level event delegation with closest()
to ensure fullscreen/close buttons work regardless of DOM position.

commit.message:
fix: address critical and major issues from comprehensive testing

Critical:
- Fix XSS in HTML report onclick handler (escJs instead of esc)
- Fix MCP logger corrupting stdout JSON-RPC (route to stderr)

Major:
- Log skipped conversations in triage_check instead of silent catch
- Add missing axiom/langfuse check in triage_explain trace source gate
- Exclude truncationScore from averageMetrics (binary flag skewed quality)
- Add zero-conversations guard in CLI analyzeCommand
- Log recommendation generation failures in MCP tools

Minor:
- Clamp rootCauseTurn to valid range in diagnosis and MCP helpers
- Use validateEnum in individualCheck (matching batchCheck)
- Add readOnlyHint annotations to 4 read-only MCP tools
- Expand test mock conversation to match rootCauseTurn fixture

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: agent-triage
repository.description: Diagnose your AI agents in production. Extract policies from prompts, evaluate traces, generate diagnostic reports.

commit.message:
Fix phantom permission notifications for auto-approved tools

Skip writing the pending marker file for tools that are always
auto-approved (Read, Glob, Grep, ToolSearch) and increase the
Hammerspoon debounce from 1s to 2s. This prevents false
focus/notification triggers for tools that don't actually need
user permission.

Also syncs embedded Hammerspoon config with live bertrand.lua
(registration persistence across reloads).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: shared ESM-aware module resolution (#461)

Extract duplicate resolveModulePath into a shared utility that prefers
ESM entry points. Uses require.resolve() then checks for a .mjs sibling
file, which covers the common dual-publish pattern (index.js + index.mjs).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: babel-plugin-javascript-aot
repository.description: AOT transpiler for JavaScript for non-hot path code optimization.

commit.message:
fix: DOM tracker test timing flakes — daysAgo() now uses stable offset

The daysAgo() helper was creating dates with sub-millisecond timing
that caused Math.floor(elapsed / MS_PER_DAY) to round down by 1.
Fixed by offsetting 1 hour and reusing the same Date reference.

All 57 compliance tests now pass (was 53/57).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Increase cursor size by 10%

Resize all cursor PNGs using nearest-neighbor to preserve pixel art.
Arrows/hands: 12x17 → 13x19, text: 7x17 → 8x19, wait: 11x13 → 12x14.
